title: The per-call dollar cost is in spidergate_metadata.cost_usd, not the usage block
summary: Agents reflexively read response.usage for cost — but usage is token COUNTS only (prompt/completion/total_tokens). The actual dollars for THIS call live in response.spidergate_metadata.cost_usd, priced on the model that actually served (provider_model), which can differ from the alias slot-0 when a fallback fired (fallback_used / original_model). The aggregate /api/v1/brands/{brand_id}/gate/usage rollup is batched and brand-keyed — don't poll it for a call you just made; read the metadata off the response. cache_hit:true → cost_usd:0.
references: app/api/v1/gate/schemas.py — SpiderGateMetadata (provider, provider_model, cost_usd, latency_ms, cache_hit, fallback_used, original_model) vs TokenUsage (tokens only)
docs/services/SpiderGate/CLAUDE.md — 'Response carries spidergate_metadata { provider, latency_ms, cache_hit, cost_usd }'
